Save video Astronomy The history of astronomy is a history of conjuring intelligent life where it isn’t 34 minutes - Aeon
Save essay Neuroscience How to make a map of smell We can split light by a prism, sounds by tones, but surely the world of odour is too complex and personal? Strangely, no Jason Castro - Aeon
Save essay Thinkers and theories Philosophy is an art For Margaret Macdonald, philosophical theories are akin to stories, meant to enlarge certain aspects of human life Peter West - Aeon
Save video Rituals and celebrations A beginner’s guide to a joyful Persian tradition of spring renewal and rebirth 3 minutes - Aeon

Save video Music The peculiar beauty of a song caught between composition and improvisation 3 minutes - Aeon
Save essay Knowledge What is ‘lived experience’? The term is ubiquitous and double-edged. It is both a key source of authentic knowledge and a danger to true solidarity Patrick J Casey - Aeon
Save video Cosmology Tiny, entangled universes that form or fizzle out – a theory of the quantum multiverse 11 minutes - Aeon
Save essay Metaphysics The enchanted vision Love is much more than a mere emotion or moral ideal. It imbues the world itself and we should learn to move with its power Mark Vernon - Aeon
Save essay Economic history Credit card nation Americans have always borrowed, but how exactly did their lives become so entangled with the power of plastic cards? Sean H Vanatta - Aeon
Save video Rituals and celebrations A whale hunt is an act of prayer for an Inuit community north of the Arctic Circle 8 minutes - Aeon

Save essay The environment Emergency action Could civil disobedience be morally obligatory in a society on a collision course with climate catastrophe? Rupert Read - Aeon
Save essay Ethics The scourge of lookism It is time to take seriously the painful consequences of appearance discrimination in the workplace Andrew Mason - Aeon
Save essay Thinkers and theories Our tools shape our selves For Bernard Stiegler, a visionary philosopher of our digital age, technics is the defining feature of human experience Bryan Norton - Aeon
Save essay History of technology Why America fell for guns The US today has extraordinary levels of gun ownership. But to see this as a venerable tradition is to misread history Megan Kang - Aeon

Save essay Psychiatry and psychotherapy The therapist who hated me Going to a child psychoanalyst four times a week for three years was bad enough. Reading what she wrote about me was worse Michael Bacon - Aeon
Save essay History What would Thucydides say? In constantly reaching for past parallels to explain our peculiar times we miss the real lessons of the master historian Mark Fisher - Aeon
